name: pyscitt

metadata.name: pyscitt
metadata.summary: Tools to sign claims and interact with a SCITT CCF Ledger

documentation:
# pyscitt: Python CLI tools for SCITT CCF Ledger

Tools to sign claims and interact with a SCITT CCF Ledger.

For more information, please find the `scitt-ccf-ledger` repository at https://github.com/microsoft/scitt-ccf-ledger.

Package sources are available at https://github.com/microsoft/scitt-ccf-ledger/tree/main/pyscitt.

modules.name: pyscitt.did
documentation: Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation.
Licensed under the MIT License.

functions.name: pyscitt.did.get_verification_method_kid
documentation: The "kid" COSE header parameter refers
to the fragment of the verification method's ID.
The part before the fragment is the DID itself
and stored in "issuer".

Licensed under the MIT License.

classes.name: pyscitt.key_vault_sign_client.KeyVaultSignClient

methods.name: pyscitt.key_vault_sign_client.KeyVaultSignClient.cose_sign
documentation:
Generates a COSE payload for the specified request with the specified headers.

<https://microsoft.github.io/CCF/main/use_apps/issue_commands.html#signing>

```
param data

The intended body for the HTTP request.

type data

bytes

param cose_headers

The headers to include in the COSE payload.

type cose_headers

Optional[Dict]

return

The full payload, with signature, to be sent to CCF.

rtype

bytes
```

methods.name: pyscitt.key_vault_sign_client.KeyVaultSignClient.http_sign
documentation:
Generates a HTTP signing payload for the specified request with the specified headers.

<https://microsoft.github.io/CCF/main/use_apps/issue_commands.html#signing>

```
param data

The intended body for the HTTP request.

type data

bytes

return

The full payload, with signature, to be sent to CCF.

rtype

bytes
```
MemberIdentity implementation that uses Azure Key Vault.

Licensed under the MIT License.

methods.name: pyscitt.verify.ServiceParameters.from_dict
documentation: Decode service parameters as returned by the /parameters endpoint.

methods.name: pyscitt.verify.TrustStore.lookup
documentation:
Look up a service's parameters based on the protected headers from a
receipt. Raises an exception if not matching service is found.

classes.name: pyscitt.verify.StaticTrustStore

methods.name: pyscitt.verify.StaticTrustStore.load
documentation:
Populate a static trust store from a directory. Each JSON file in the
directory corresponds to a trusted service identity.
A static trust store, based on a list of trusted service certificates.

classes.name: pyscitt.verify.DIDDocumentTrustStore
documentation:
A trust store backed by a single DID-document.

The trust store will use the KID found in the protected headers to select
the appropriate assertion method from the document.

classes.name: pyscitt.verify.DIDResolverTrustStore
documentation:
A trust store which uses the issuer found in receipts to dynamically
resolve the service parameters.

The trust store does not restrict which issuers are allowed, only that the
receipt signature matches the identifier.

functions.name: pyscitt.cli.client_arguments.add_client_arguments
documentation:
Add command-line arguments to an argparse parser, such that a client instance can be configured.

with_member_auth:
    If True, required flag arguments for a member certificate and private key will be added
    to the parser.

with_auth_token:
    If True, an optional flag argument allowing a bearer token to be specified will be added
    to the parser.

development_only:
    If True, the server's TLS certificate is never verified, and no --development flag is added
    to the parser.

functions.name: pyscitt.cli.client_arguments.create_client
documentation:
Create a client instance from the result of parsing command-line arguments.

This assumes the argument parser was configured with the `add_client_arguments` function.

classes.name: pyscitt.client.HttpSig
documentation: copied from CCF/tests/infra/clients.py

classes.name: pyscitt.client.OffSettableSecondsSinceEpoch
documentation: When running functional tests, we may send identical proposals that,
if signed within the same second, they may hit the ProposalReplay
protection error from CCF.
We use a custom clock that can be manually advanced, same as
implemented by CCF, to avoid sleeping for 1 second between
identical proposals.
Source: https://github.com/microsoft/CCF/blob/d6efe6664045968dc4f191b9ae672e686f05279b/tests/infra/clients.py#L40

classes.name: pyscitt.client.BaseClient

methods.name: pyscitt.client.BaseClient.__init__
documentation:
Create a new BaseClient instance.

auth_token:
    A bearer token for all requests made by this instance.

member_auth:
    MemberAuthenticationMethod include A pair of certificate and private key in PEM format or AKV login identity, used to sign requests.
    Each request that needs signing must also be given the `sign_request=True` parameter.

wait_time:
    The time to wait between retries. If None, the default wait time is used.

development:
    If true, the TLS certificate of the server will not be verified.

cacert:
    If set and development is False, will be used in TLS verification instead of the default bundle.

methods.name: pyscitt.client.BaseClient.replace
documentation:
Create a new instance with certain parameters modified. Any parameters
that weren't specified will be inherited from the current instance.

The accepted keyword arguments are the same as those of the constructor.

methods.name: pyscitt.client.BaseClient.request
documentation:
Issue a request to the server.

retry_on: a list of predicates on the response.
    Each predicate can be one of an HTTP status code, a pair of a status code and a error
    code or a callable that accepts a response object and returns True if the request
    should be retried. If any predicate matches, the request is retried after a short delay.

sign_request:
    Sign the request with the member private key. A key and certificate must have been
    provided to the constructor.

wait_for_confirmation:
    Wait for the transaction created by this request to be globally committed. If True, the
    client will wait for confirmation and raise an error if the transaction was rolled-back.
    Otherwise, any write to the key-value store caused by this request could be silently
    rolled-back.

Other keyword-arguments are passed to httpx.

methods.name: pyscitt.client.BaseClient.wait_for_confirmation
documentation:
Wait until a transaction has either been globally committed or was rolled back.

In the latter case, an exception is raised.

methods.name: pyscitt.client.BaseClient.get_historical
documentation:
Issue a request, retrying on codes commonly used by CCF applications to indicate that a
historical query to the KV is in progress and needs to be retried.

Wrapper around an HTTP client, with facilities to interact with a CCF-based
service.

Provides support for authentication, request signing, logging, configurable
retry conditions, etc...

classes.name: pyscitt.client.PendingSubmission
documentation: The pending result of submitting a statement to the service.

classes.name: pyscitt.client.Submission

methods.name: pyscitt.client.Submission.seqno
documentation: Extract the sequence number from the transaction ID.

methods.name: pyscitt.client.Submission.receipt
documentation: Parse the receipt bytes and return a Receipt object.
The result of submitting a statement to the service.

classes.name: pyscitt.client.Client

methods.name: pyscitt.client.Client.get_receipt
documentation:
Get a receipt from the ledger.

If `operation` is true, the tx is treated as an operation ID and is
first waited on in order to obtain the actual entry ID.

methods.name: pyscitt.client.Client.get_transparent_statement
documentation:
Get a transparent statement from the ledger.

If `operation` is true, the tx is treated as an operation ID and is
first waited on in order to obtain the actual entry ID.

methods.name: pyscitt.client.Client.enumerate_statements
documentation:
Enumerate all statements on the ledger, with an optional start and end range.

Yields a sequence of transaction numbers. The contents and/or receipt for a given claim can
be fetched using the `get_claim` and `get_receipt` methods.
Specialization of the BaseClient, aimed at interacting with a SCITT CCF ledger instance.

functions.name: pyscitt.cli.governance.clean_pem_file

modules.name: pyscitt.governance
documentation: Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation.
Licensed under the MIT License.

methods.name: pyscitt.governance.GovernanceClient.propose
documentation:
Submit a proposal to the SCITT CCF instance.

Depending on the instance's constitution, proposals may not pass immediately and may need
more members to cast a favourable ballot. The caller should either check this method's
return value or use the `must_pass` argument before assuming the proposal is effective.

vote:
    If True and the proposal isn't automatically accepted, an unconditional vote in its
    favour is cast.

must_pass:
    If True and the proposal fails to pass even after a ballot in its favour is cast, an
    exception is raised.

methods.name: pyscitt.governance.GovernanceClient.recover_service
documentation:
Perform a disaster recovery of the service. This assumes the service
has a single member.

https://microsoft.github.io/CCF/release/3.x/operations/recovery.html
https://microsoft.github.io/CCF/release/3.x/governance/accept_recovery.html

functions.name: pyscitt.crypto.default_algorithm_for_key
documentation:
Get the default algorithm for a given key, based on its
type and parameters.

functions.name: pyscitt.crypto.embed_receipt_in_cose
documentation: Append the receipt to an unprotected header in a COSE_Sign1 message.

functions.name: pyscitt.crypto.get_last_embedded_receipt_from_cose
documentation: Extract the last receipt from the unprotected header of a COSE_Sign1 message.

functions.name: pyscitt.crypto.sign_statement
documentation: TODO: merge with Key Vault signer implementation

functions.name: pyscitt.crypto.decode_p1363_signature
documentation:
Decode an ECDSA signature from its IEEE P1363 encoding into its r and s
components. The two integers are padded to the curve size and concatenated.

This is the format used throughout the COSE/JOSE ecosystem.

functions.name: pyscitt.crypto.convert_p1363_signature_to_dss
documentation:
Convert an ECDSA signature from its IEEE P1363 encoding to an ASN1/DER
encoding.

The former is the format used throughout the COSE/JOSE ecosystem. The
latter is used by OpenSSL and cryptography, as well as the CCF python
module.

functions.name: pyscitt.crypto.decrypt_recovery_share
documentation:
Decrypt a CCF recovery share, using the member's encryption private key.

https://microsoft.github.io/CCF/release/3.x/governance/accept_recovery.html

variables.name: pyscitt.crypto.CWT_SVN
documentation: AMD Security Version Number

classes.name: pyscitt.crypto.CWTClaims
documentation: Include SCITT-specific COSE header attributes to be recognized by pycose
Registered COSE header labels are in https://www.iana.org/assignments/cose/cose.xhtml
Draft SCITT-specific header labels are in https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/draft-ietf-scitt-architecture/

methods.name: pyscitt.crypto.Signer.__init__
documentation: If no algorithm is specified, a sensible default is inferred from the private key.
